_doc: Gold-FAIL exemplars: rewrites that a naive judge might reward but that must FAIL, paired with the dimension they violate. A detection category is only safe to ship WITH the exemplar of over-applying it -- otherwise the category becomes a license to inject the very fault it claims to remove. Fields mirror the eval-hardening branch's FAIL_REQUIRED schema so a merge is a pure array-append: `id`, `genre`, `difficulty`, `before` (a genuinely fine text -- NOT a tell), `after` (an OVER-CORRECTION that manufactured a fault to look human), `rubric_focus` (dimensions the judge is told to weigh -- `fail_dimension` must appear here unless it is `no_fabrication`, which the rubric always requires), `expected_verdict: FAIL` (gold label), `fail_dimension` (the rubric dimension whose validity condition the `after` violates), and `why` (one-line defect explanation). Optional `before_band_max`/`after_band_max` are loose detector regression guards (these strings are not detector-targeted -- both score near 0 -- the band just stops them silently rotting into vocabulary tells). These are NOT loaded by run_fixtures.py's PASS-set calibration path (they would break the 30-40% band and the all-PASS gold set in fixtures.json); they are a documented judge-discrimination contract, mirroring how the over-stepping FAIL exemplars live in test_fixtures.py::OversteppingHardNegatives rather than in the PASS fixture set.

fixtures.before: The nightly backup job is green again. The fix was a one-character typo in the cron schedule.
fixtures.after: The nightly backup job is green again, for now. The fix was a one-character typo in the cron schedule, though I keep wondering whether the real story is messier, whether something deeper in how we think about scheduling is unresolved, and whether fixed is ever really the right word.
fixtures.why: OVER-CORRECTION of the narrative-shape category. The `before` is a genuinely simple, single-outcome update: the thread is truly closed (a one-character cron typo, now green). Its tidy resolution is MOTIVATED (validity condition) -> NOT a tell. The `after` manufactures fake residue and fake ambiguity ('for now', 'the real story is messier', 'whether fixed is ever really the right word') purely to LOOK human -- injected complexity where the material supplies none. A naive judge might reward 'it left a thread open, good'; the validity condition forbids that. narrative_shape_ok: FAIL (over-correction). This is the failure mode the category must ship paired with, exactly as over-stepping ships its 不是X而是Y over-strip hard-negatives.
